notes: A FHIR-like Patient can become a PERSON row after de-identification and source-value governance.
Numeric FHIR-like Observation.valueQuantity rows are previewed as MEASUREMENT rows.
Non-numeric FHIR-like Observation rows remain OBSERVATION candidates.
Condition rows need vocabulary review before analytics export.
Medication resources require RxNorm/RxNav candidate review before export.
Encounter rows can become VISIT_OCCURRENCE rows after visit concept and date checks.
Document and report text should be redacted and approved before NOTE export.

field_mappings.transformation: copy source identifier as governed source value
notes: Do not infer a stable person_id in the preview layer.
Source text remains visible even when standard concept mapping is missing.

field_mappings.transformation: copy date-like value without timezone inference
notes: Dates must pass validation before production analytics export.
Rows without numeric value are routed to OBSERVATION by the row rule.
UCUM validation and unit_concept_id mapping remain review-gated.

field_mappings.transformation: resolve reviewed LOINC/RxNorm/SNOMED candidate to OMOP standard concept
notes: The preview never guesses OMOP concept IDs when vocabulary evidence is missing.
Non-numeric observations need concept review before research use.
Condition concepts should resolve through SNOMED or ICD vocabulary review.
Medication concepts should resolve through RxNorm/RxNav review.
Visit type and date completeness remain required before analytics export.

field_mappings.transformation: copy governed artifact reference, not raw note text
notes: Raw document content must pass redaction and approval before NOTE export.

review_policy: OMOP export preview is advisory. Production ETL requires vocabulary resolution, de-identification review, DQD validation, and data-steward approval.
standard_refs: OHDSI OMOP Common Data Model: https://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/
warnings: This profile creates preview counts and governance warnings; it does not produce production OMOP SQL loads.
OMOP concept IDs must come from a reviewed vocabulary release, not from model inference.
